title: Failure running podman with systemd as non root user
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I'm trying to run a a systemd service with podman as non privileged user but the service fails with  error. The same service work when executed as root

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create a new user 
```
useradd -s /sbin/nologin --create-home nexus-system
```

2. install a systemd service file as follow:
```
[Unit]
Description=Nexus Podman container
Wants=syslog.service

[Service]
User=nexus-system
Group=nexus-system
Restart=always
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run \
	--log-level=debug \
	--rm \
	-ti \
	--publish 8081:8081 \
	--name nexus \
	sonatype/nexus3:3.20.1

ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 nexus

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
3. run the service
```
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl start nexus
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Service fails to start with the following message:
```
could not get runtime: error generating default config from memory: cannot mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: no such file or directory
```

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.6
  podman version: 1.8.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.13.1
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.10-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.10, commit: 6b526d9888abb86b9e7de7dfdeec0da98ad32ee0'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  MemFree: 32241123328
  MemTotal: 33633120256
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.12.1-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.12.1
      commit: df5f2b2369b3d9f36d175e1183b26e5cee55dd0a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 34359734272
  SwapTotal: 34359734272
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: uranus
  kernel: 5.4.17-200.fc31.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 38m 18.26s
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.8.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body:
could not get runtime: error generating default config from memory: cannot mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: no such file or directory
This looks like you have hacked up the ~/.config/containers directory?

I think we select that directory if all else fails and we cannot find a usable /run/user or /tmp

In any case, having /run in your $HOME and not on a tmpfs is DANGEROUS and strongly not recommended. You may wish to run `podman system reset` (will delete all containers + pods + images) and let Podman regenerate its configuration - that may get a more usable tmpdir

> could not get runtime: error generating default config from memory: cannot mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: mkdir /home/nexus-system/rundir/libpod: no such file or directory
> This looks like you have hacked up the ~/.config/containers directory?

Not at all

> I think we select that directory if all else fails and we cannot find a usable /run/user or /tmp
> 
> In any case, having /run in your $HOME and not on a tmpfs is DANGEROUS and strongly not recommended. You may wish to run `podman system reset` (will delete all containers + pods + images) and let Podman regenerate its configuration - that may get a more usable tmpdir

`podman system reset` done, but I see the same error
The issue here is the user has never logged, in.  Podman is being run as a user, but podman makes assumptions about XDG_RUNTIME_DIR being setup when it executes.  I think this is what is going wrong.
so `loginctl enable-linger nexus-system` seems to help here
